Theorem 2 is also too compressed in the Main theorems section to serve as a standalone theorem statement.

novelty.notes: The derivation is below the enforced flagship floor. The critical-radius result is largely a support-face identity induced by the definition of eta_w^*(P), and the two-shock result is a finite-dimensional angular specialization; the note does not establish a new named-estimator frontier or a published open-regime extension at flagship depth.

review.verbatim_critique: The artifact is structurally close to a usable Stage 0 note, but it needs revision before it can pass the requested flagship gate. The largest structural problem is cluster drift: the run metadata declares exactid, while the locked block in Formal setup, lines 191-201, declares a PartialID support-function structural time-series anchor. The mathematical content is in fact PartialID-style support-function geometry, not exact identification by SWIG, adjustment, or do-calculus. If this is the intended anchor, the run metadata and downstream target should be reconciled; if exactid is intended, the derivation must be re-derived around an identifying functional and assumptions appropriate to that cluster. Theorem thm:critical-leakage-frontier is mathematically plausible as a compactness result, but its main equivalence follows almost immediately from defining eta_w^*(P) as the first leakage radius at which the exposed LP support face intersects the contact set. That makes the current kernel useful but not flagship-grade. To meet the flagship target, the note would need a genuine comparative theorem, for example an if-and-only-if frontier between named published LP/sign and higher-cumulant SVAR procedures, or a strict extension of a published open class, not only a rephrasing of support equality as support-face contact. The positive open semi-algebraic class claim in lines 414-435 also needs more detail: the displayed two-shock center is asserted to arise from a stable finite-order VAR with the chosen cumulants, and the persistence/genericity argument is compressed into strict-inequality and Tarski-Seidenberg language without spelling out the actual quantified semi-algebraic condition. Theorem thm:two-shock-formula is especially under-stated in lines 308-311. The precise formula appears earlier and in the proof, but the main theorem statement should include the angular parametrization, the pure-diagonal feasibility branch, the residual minimization, and the component-to-global rule. The proof at lines 466-472 also needs an explicit rotation/reflection parametrization to justify that the two components preserve the support value and off-diagonal residual norm under the fixed shock label. The related-work section is targeted and cites relevant literatures, but the theorem-level positioning remains too generic for the requested flagship novelty: it does not name the closest published theorem or estimator frontier that this result strictly extends or sharply separates.

review.verbatim_critique:
The note is structurally readable and includes the required sections, labelled assumptions, a computable program in the estimand-defining functional section, sanity checks, minimality checks, and a usable math-objects checklist. The first blocking issue is anchor drift: the run metadata supplies an exactid cluster, exactid substrate roots, and exactid reuse list, but the locked-parameter block in Formal setup declares a PartialID support-function structural time-series problem. The later counterexample section explicitly says the shared setup is PartialID even though the run metadata names exact-ID. Under the review rules this is a structure failure requiring re-derivation under the declared cluster or a corrected locked-parameter scope.

The main mathematical problem is more serious. The critical-frontier theorem defines eta_w^*(P) as the infimum of leakage radii for which the exposed LP maximizer set intersects Q_eta(P), then proves that support equality holds exactly when eta is at least that value. Once Q_eta(P) is nested, closed, and compact enough for suprema to be attained, this is a generic support-face lemma. It does not establish a new identifying formula in the econometric sense; it packages the equality condition into the definition of the threshold. The proof at lines 370-400 makes this explicit by reducing equality to M_w(P) intersecting Q_eta(P), then identifying the set of admissible radii from the definition of eta_w^*(P). This violates the correctness gate against theorem conclusions that are definition-body restatements, and it also prevents flagship novelty.

The positive finite open semi-algebraic class claim is not fully justified. The displayed two-shock center is plausible as a finite-dimensional algebraic witness, but the note does not construct an actual population law with the stated VAR response rows and fourth cumulant tensor, nor does it prove that the regular-frontier assumption holds beyond asserting zero-dimensional exposure and a scalar boundary condition. The Tarski-Seidenberg paragraph also treats the exposed support value and support maximizer as algebraic without spelling out the universal inequalities needed to certify global maximization over O(n). These are repairable mathematical gaps for a modest theorem, but they do not rescue the artifact as a flagship derivation.

The two-shock theorem is better as a computation, but its theorem statement is too compressed: the actual componentwise formula appears in the introduction and proof, while the theorem site only says the answer is a minimum residual over support angles. Even if expanded, it is a specialization of the same definition of eta_w^*(P), not a comparator-chain frontier between named published estimators or a strict extension of a published open class. The related-work section gives useful relevance sentences, but it does not name a specific prior theorem or estimator workflow whose identified set is sharply generalized by the result.

Because novelty_target is flagship, the artifact cannot be accepted at the derivation tier it actually proves. A revised Stage 0 would need either a non-definitional flagship theorem under the correct locked cluster, or a narrower target with the tier floor lowered. For the present artifact, the delivered kernel is a generic nested-contact support threshold plus a two-shock specialization, not a flagship contribution.

review.verbatim_critique: The note has all required Stage 0 sections and the Section 14 checklist is usable, but it does not preserve the run anchor: the brief declares the exactid cluster, while the locked block in Formal setup declares a PartialID/support-function structural time-series anchor. That must be corrected before downstream stages can rely on the locked tuple. Mathematically, the central argument in the proof of Conjecture 1 reduces to a generic compactness fact: for a subset Q_eta of Q_LP, the support functions are equal exactly when an LP maximizer belongs to Q_eta, and eta_w^* is defined as the first leakage radius at which that intersection occurs. This is sound under the stated compactness and nonemptiness caveats, but it is not a flagship theorem-level contribution. The two-shock theorem is likewise the angular specialization of the same definition, taking the residual norm over exposed angles satisfying diagonal contact. The theorem statements should also surface the feasibility and infinite-radius branches that the proofs rely on. The positive open semi-algebraic example needs a fuller validity check that the proposed fourth-cumulant tensor is actually attainable by an innovation law compatible with the finite-order VAR population, not merely algebraically written as a tensor. The related-work section is targeted, but it does not name a published open problem, estimator frontier, or diagnose-then-correct target that this theorem resolves. Under the flagship novelty floor, the current derivation is a useful support-function formalization but not a flagship-level kernel.

review.verbatim_critique:
The note is not viable as the requested exactid Stage 0 artifact. The run metadata declares the exactid cluster, but the locked-parameter block at lines 190-200 identifies a PartialID/support-function structural time-series problem, and the counterexample section at lines 550-551 explicitly says the shared setup is PartialID despite the exact-ID run metadata. That is locked-parameter drift and should be repaired before later stages consume the file.

The main mathematical kernel is also below the requested flagship floor. Theorem critical-leakage-frontier at lines 302-305 says equality holds exactly when eta exceeds eta_w^*(P), but eta_w^*(P) is defined at lines 267-270 as the first eta for which the LP maximizer set intersects Q_eta(P). The proof at lines 370-402 is then the standard fact that optimizing over a subset has the same support value exactly when that subset contains an outer-set maximizer. This is a useful bookkeeping identity, not a new theorem-level contribution relative to the cited LP/SVAR and sign-restriction literature.

The novelty positioning therefore overstates what has been derived. The related-work section correctly cites LP/VAR equivalence, sign-restricted SVAR geometry, non-Gaussian SVAR identification, and higher-moment fragility, but the note does not prove a new exact-identification formula, a sharp new bound beyond the support-face definition, or a named estimator frontier involving at least two published estimators. The operational scalar eta_w^*(P) is computable, but its role is definitional: it is the residual radius needed for an exposed maximizer to enter the smaller contact set.

Several proof details need repair even if the artifact is re-scoped as PartialID. The open semi-algebraic positive-frontier argument at lines 414-434 asserts that the two-shock center is realized by a stable VAR with the specified response rows and cumulants, and that small perturbations preserve the relevant exposed support structure and regular isolated-boundary condition. Those are not derived from the earlier assumptions. The two-shock formula theorem at lines 308-311 is too terse as a theorem statement; the actual minimization formula appears earlier in the conjecture text, not in the theorem. The parent-corner lower bound at lines 474-482 assumes a same-interval monotone branch relationship between the LP support angle and the contact angle; that branch condition is stronger than the stated two-shock assumptions and should be made explicit or the bound should be weakened.

The formalization checklist at lines 570-596 is largely actionable and separates the finite-dimensional rotation, cumulant, support-function, and leakage objects. The structural issue is not the checklist; it is the mismatch between the declared cluster and the artifact's PartialID object, combined with the fact that the headline theorem is essentially the definition of a first-contact radius rather than a flagship derivation.

reason: Case 6b — boundary stage_0.5_to_0 with status reject on conjecture-level grounds: cluster mismatch (exactid declared but locked-parameter block at lines 190-200 anchors a PartialID/support-function problem, explicitly acknowledged at lines 550-551), and the headline Theorem critical-leakage-frontier (lines 302-305, proof 370-402) is a definitional unfold of eta_w^*(P) (lines 267-270) rather than a flagship result. Reviewer's tier verdict is tier_genuinely_below. These are not localized derivation slips a Stage 0 re-derive can fix — the conjecture as stated either belongs to a different cluster or collapses to its own definition. Re-deriving on the same .tex would burn budget.
proposed_action: Pivot to a new proposal angle: the current upgrade2 has (i) cluster/anchor inconsistency between exactid run metadata and a PartialID support-function object, and (ii) a headline equivalence that reduces to the definition of the first-contact leakage radius eta_w^*(P). Stage -1 should redraft either as a genuine exactid frontier (a named-estimator non-equivalence or a sharp identification regime beyond support-face bookkeeping) or re-cluster to partialid with a substantive support-function frontier that is not a first-contact-radius tautology.

structure.notes: The skeleton sections are present and the checklist is actionable, but the locked-parameter block declares a PartialID support-function anchor while the run metadata declares the exactid cluster; the main theorem statements in Section 9 are also too compressed to carry the displayed formulas and exceptional branches needed downstream.

novelty.notes: The derived kernel is a generic support-face contact identity once eta_w^*(P) is defined as the first radius at which the LP maximizer set intersects Q_eta(P). It does not establish a flagship-level equivalence or non-equivalence frontier between named published estimators or identification strategies.

correctness.notes: The basic compactness argument for the frontier is largely sound, but some claimed strengthening is underproved, especially the nonempty open semi-algebraic class and the two-component O(2) reduction as stated in the theorem rather than only in the proof.

perItemFindings.one_line: The equivalence h_LP=h_eta iff eta >= eta_w^*(P) follows almost immediately from defining eta_w^*(P) as the first leakage level at which the LP support face intersects Q_eta(P), so the current derivation is below the enforced flagship floor.

perItemFindings.label: Proof of Conjecture 1
perItemFindings.one_line: The proof of a positive finite frontier on a nonempty open semi-algebraic class relies on a single constructed center and informal perturbation claims rather than a complete certificate that the advertised observable class is nonempty and open under all stated restrictions.

perItemFindings.one_line: The global minimum over rotation and reflection components is asserted using an angular relabelling and norm-preservation argument that is plausible in two dimensions but not stated with enough hypotheses in the theorem itself.

review.verbatim_critique:
The artifact is structurally close to a reviewable Stage 0 note, with the required sections, labelled assumptions, support-function definitions, sanity checks, counterexample/minimality checks, and a useful math-object checklist. The first problem is anchor drift. The brief places this run in the exactid cluster, but the locked block in Formal setup declares a PartialID support-function structural time-series anchor, and the later counterexample section explicitly says the shared setup is PartialID despite the exact-ID run metadata. Because the note is in fact written as a PartialID support-function theorem, later exactid-specific stages would not know whether to formalize do-calculus-style identification or algebraic support bounds.

The main mathematical derivation is not flagship-level. The displayed critical radius is defined as the first eta for which the exposed LP maximizer set intersects the eta-contact set. With nested closed contact sets, compactness, and support attainment, the equality h_LP=h_eta iff that intersection is nonempty is a standard support-function observation. The proof of Conjecture 1 then repackages this definition as the headline frontier. That can be a useful organizing lemma, but it is not a new equivalence frontier between named published estimators or identification strategies, nor does it resolve an open non-Gaussian SVAR identification regime. The cited literature is relevant, but the comparison remains generic: LP/sign relaxation versus higher-cumulant contact set. It does not identify two named estimators or workflows and prove a new sharp threshold separating their equivalence and non-equivalence regimes.

The theorem statements in Main theorems need to carry the actual content. Theorem 1 says the conjecture is confirmed and mentions positivity and finiteness, but the operative definition, feasibility convention, and nonempty-contact qualification are outside the statement. Theorem 2 is only a prose sentence, while the formula with the plus-infinity branch and componentwise minimization appears earlier and in the proof. For downstream use, the statement itself should expose the component formula, the pure-diagonal feasibility branch, and the global aggregation over O(2).

On correctness, the core compactness argument for the equality frontier is mostly recoverable. The weakest part is the advertised nonempty open semi-algebraic class. The proof gives one two-shock center and says perturbations preserve the required inequalities and regular isolated-boundary condition after removing an exceptional set. That is not yet a complete certificate that the claimed class of observable VAR/cumulant objects is nonempty, open, and semi-algebraic under the stated population restrictions. The two-shock proof also needs a sharper statement of the hypotheses under which reflection only relabels or sign-changes off-diagonal fourth-cumulant coordinates while preserving the residual norm and the LP support value.

A repair aimed at the current theorem would likely produce a sound subfield or field-level support-function lemma. To meet the enforced flagship target, the note would need a genuinely stronger kernel: for example, a threshold theorem tied to named published LP and SVAR identification procedures, with an if-and-only-if frontier that is not merely induced by the definition of the critical radius, or a generic-class result showing a new open regime for higher-cumulant SVAR support identification.
